question_title: What will be the value of "1-Year Treasury Bill Secondary Market Rate, Discount Basis" on 2026-02-26?
summary: The 1-Year Treasury Bill rate (DTB1YR) was 3.33% on Feb 12, 2026 (latest data). The rate has been range-bound between 3.28-3.42% over the past month with near-zero daily drift (-0.0004%/day). No FOMC meeting occurs before Feb 26 (next is March 17-18), so no major policy catalyst exists. Monte Carlo simulation using bootstrapped empirical daily changes from Nov 2025-Feb 2026 data, blending recent (30-day) and full-period behavior, produces a median forecast of ~3.31% with moderate uncertainty driven by normal market volatility over a ~10 trading day horizon.

factors.description: Current rate is 3.33%, range-bound between 3.28-3.42% for past month with near-zero drift
No FOMC meeting before Feb 26 - removes primary catalyst for large rate moves
Slight longer-term downward drift (-0.004%/day over 3 months) from Fed easing cycle, though pace has slowed significantly
Fed funds rate at 3.64%, 1-yr bill at 3.33% already pricing in further easing - relationship stable
Potential for economic data releases (CPI, jobs) to shift rate expectations by 3-6bp
